threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when you hear someone ask if you want a 'half' on your drink – it’s not about measurements here.
Caught a lady on the 59 last night, singing old Motown hits. She had us all in stitches, but the driver just kept a straight face.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a group of teens joyfully transforming a vacant lot into a pop-up garden, sharing beer and laughter under a graffiti sunset.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find a former airport turned park where you can spot rollerblading seniors racing against kite surfers on a Saturday afternoon.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a real local by how they know to avoid Dolores Park on weekends; the blanket chaos is a dead giveaway for tourists.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 3rd Muni bus in a row that had a wild saxophonist jammin’ in the back. Only in Bayview can that be the morning commute soundtrack.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí and Plaza del Sol, but the real soul is in the forgotten streets where the old trades still quietly persist.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they fumble with the metro map, still figuring out that L5 connects to nowhere during rush hour.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You mention getting your coffee from Pret while standing in line at the local bakery. The side-eye you'll get is priceless.
Last week on the 333, a guy serenaded the whole bus with old soul tracks. Never thought I'd see Brixton's night life spill over like that!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a hipster on a bike dodging a street vendor selling traditional Turkish gözleme—only in Neukölln, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You know those spontaneous outdoor chess games? In Friedrichshain parks, they turn into local exhibitions with crowds cheering every brilliant move.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a true local when they know to avoid the 24th St BART at 5 PM like it’s a zombie apocalypse, not just rush hour.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw an artist on the 31 Balboa, sketching the faces of sleeping passengers. The bus turned into a gallery for a brief moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think they can just walk into the local bars and expect to be served right away. Here, a friendly smile and a moment of patience go a long way.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their puzzled looks at Gràcia's winding streets; they still think GPS can guide them through the maze.
